title: Health insurance claims cannot be rejected citing time bar clauses in the policy: Bombay HC
description: The Bombay High Court has held that insurers cannot reject health insurance claims solely on the ground of delay where a policy-imposed time bar effectively extinguishes the insured’s right. [CP Ravindranath Menon v. United India Insurance Company].A Division Bench of Justices Bharati Dangre and Manjusha Deshpande declared that such time-bar clauses are void under Section 28(b) of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.Section 28(b) renders void any agreement that extinguishes a party’s rights or dischar
